title: Severinia lauterbachii
taxonomy: Severinia lauterbachii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Severinia, which consists of a small group of species. This plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Severinia lauterbachii is also known by its synonyms, which include Zanthoxylum lauterbachii and Zanthoxylum stellatilimbum. The classification of Severinia lauterbachii is subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Severinia lauterbachii, also known as Chinese box myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Severinia lauterbachii include essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other inflammatory conditions.
